message:
fix(types): partial last block symbol count and streaming typed decoder

ObjectParams: adds test verifying that a multi-block object whose data
does not fill the last block reports the correct min_symbols_for_decode
and total_source_symbols (255, not 256 for 4 blocks of 64 with only
255 symbols worth of payload).

TypedDecoder::decode_from_stream: rewritten from collect-then-decode to
an incremental pipeline approach. Instead of collecting all typed symbols
into a Vec and decoding in batch, the stream is consumed symbol-by-symbol
with each symbol immediately fed into a DecodingPipeline. The decoder
initializes the pipeline on the first symbol's header and validates that
subsequent headers match. When the pipeline reports completion, decoding
finishes immediately without consuming the rest of the stream.

This fixes unbounded memory usage on large typed objects where the
collect-then-decode approach would buffer all symbols before starting
the decode. It also enables early termination — if the pipeline has
enough symbols to decode, remaining stream symbols are not consumed.

Tests added for the typed decoder with proper async polling infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
